I don't feel like I figured out the puzzle of when to spend stuff on revealing or leave it covered to keep Ultimate Abomination low.

Literally just for turns we got hit by master strike then a scheme twist. Didn’t get to play until turn 4

Tale of two very different games, both marred by escapes that we could do nothing about.
Game 1 was at least a 4, maybe a 5 on the Fun scale. There were cool interactions between the scheme and the villains/Mastermind, we got our stuff working, albeit slowly, and we took out Zola fairly quickly once we started hitting him.
Game 2 was some of the least fun I've ever had in this game. By turn 7 we'd had as many escapes as the first game, and they kept coming. We were both always just short of fighting anything, not helped by the first two cards I revealed in the HQ gave Zola (and the villains above them) +15 attack. We eventually were able to get him once I'd bought Ruby's Rare, and it *was* a quicker game, but not by enough to make up for the awful start.
Captain Britain was the standout hero here, very glad I picked him for both games!

Very creative setup - nice combo of scheme and mastermind. Not being able to see all of the available recruits led to a more limited deck and probably a longer game (thank goodness for sidekicks being available). We tried keeping the HQ more covered than not, to reduce abomination for all.

SUCH a frustrating match. Felt like it could have so easily swung into a quick win for the Heroes but both games dragged on with high turn counts and Strikes/Fateful Resurrection aligned to leave me with 2 escapes both games. I really thought the 2nd game was going better, but Strikes landed nearly every time P2 drew Ruby’s rare, often making it unplayable, and that game ended up going 2 turns longer than the first, even though I felt like I played it better. Got a little more PPT using Steve but the Captain Britain game was quicker so it got submitted. Looking forward to seeing someone having won this in <20 turns because the cards lined up for them. I expect I’ll be at the bottom of the pack for this one.
First attempt met a lot of bad luck shuffles, so the second is the entry but I'm pretty sure that this Hero Deck can still do better.
On the final turn, the Spider-Man 2099 / Warpath deck generated 30 Attack, which was exactly enough to fight all 3 remaining Tactics. Unreal turn, crazy synergy.

Bagel shuffle game one had all sinister out of deck by turn 9 and 10 escapes by turn 14 where I called the game lost and started over. Changed heroes both hands were doing very well. Right hand drew 8-10 cards a turn thanks to warpath. Left hand was a spider deck consistently drawing. Still couldn't keep up with the city. The bagel of all shuffles.

The beginning of this one was frantic, trying to keep the ambitions from escaping. Once I got the left side spider deck going, it became much easier. The right side went for Spiderman 2099 and agent venom, and ended up with all 3 of the high cost rares.

Knowing from my week 3 game, this was going to be another sluggish game where I will need to manage the city spaces very carefully. We wanted to combine Symbiote Spider-Man with Silk and the two drops from Warpath and Cuckoos to make a really good 2 or less deck to spin out attack early and often. 

The other side had a 3 theme deck with the Cyber Mod of Spiderman 2099 and the Cuckoo's 3 cost range card to continue to build up attack and card draw while also consistently thinning out our hero deck to get more of the 2 or less spider-friends. The Sandwoman Mastermind was no joke and slowed us down a lot as we had to keep managing the city to be able to knock her down a peg. A bit of a slow game with 31 turns but a win/moderate VP and no escapes is a win I'll take in this book.

Any game where I can use silk and agent venom I love. Additionally all the years I have owned this game I have never used the ambitions deck for any reason so it was great fun to actually have it and use it this week. This has been my favorite game I've played and we won't talk about game two.

A good, fair setup that I managed to make absolutely frustrating with the VD shuffle.  I went with Agent Venom for more Wall-crawl, more Spider triggers for Silk and earlier attack to keep the city clear.  Unfortunately I kept getting ill-timed Clones and Beasts pushing the city even with clearing Clones so before turn 8 I had all 4 escapes.  Once both sides got the 2's engine online it got a lot easier.  Comic side got all three Shadowed Spiders and got 36 attack on turn 22, took a couple turns off due to Strike complications and finished the job.

Really enjoyed this setup (I feel like I say that often). There's no easing into this one. Barely got through my opening hands and was already faced with escapes. Untimely cards in both attempts meant early escapes). Able to fend off the challenges and started getting my decks working. Moon Knight worked really well with Warpath and built the ever-popular Spidey deck on the other side. Was hoping for a little more deck-thinning but it wasn't in the cards. Ha. A really nice mix of villains and the Scheme and Mastermind had some simple but solid synergy. Both attempts I thought I could've won it sooner...but felt like things were on the verge of falling apart every couple turns. Loved it. Curious to see how my heroes stacked up against the others. Will be happy if I'm middle of the pack this week.

First time around sucked!! We have starting HQ full of 5 and 6 costs for too long and we were lucky to finish it out just to lose to the masterminds. Second time though was much better, though we took all the way to the last villain card to finish we still finished and beat the sinister 6 2099 mastermind.

Great setup challenging and fun.

Oh man, was this one tough. Just looking at it we knew that variance would hit hard and fast, and we lost the first game without touching the Mastermind. We regrouped in game two and knew we had to go hard at the Mastermind. While that strategy proved effective, we did not get punished so severely the second game, so that helped a lot. Not as fun as last week, but we are enjoying continuing to learn!

column36: We were super lucky with our villain deck getting only a few Scheme Twists, so that did not impact anything. The limited wounds were actually going to kill us. Lots of bystanders which is always nice.

Picked Soulsword colosus to deal with wounds (he also helped with Super Skrulls rare) and captain Britain because he becomes quite effective in this mix of teams. First game had too much escapes, also failed to notice Culling blade has "may" so sparned Shapeshifting copycats. Second game went better, but took long time
At first I thought burning through the Wound stack was going to be an issue to game around.  Turns out it wasn't really.

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: Really fun setup. Hiroim was an MVP with all the Bystanders flying around

Brutally Different games during both attempts. First attempt I didn't get much extra recruit at first and kept getting mostly 2 attack cards that sometimes gained Wall-Crawl from the Spider-Infected. But in this attempt the CIty felt stressful throughout the entire game. Spider-Queen's starting 8 attack value might seem laughably unimpressive, but with almost no sources of KO (at least not from Henchmen, and it felt scarce otherwise too) and therefor a lot of Starters, it took quite some time to get her Final Blow in and both the Wound Deck and Monster Unleashed Pile/Stack got close to Evil Wins.

Attempt 2 went more recruit focused at the start and the result was a much sooner victory. The City felt mostly manageable in attempt 2, but an unlucky early Marcus Symbiote Centaur pushed out 2 Villains, one of Which was Ghost Deadpool (he haunted me in my first game too...), which resulted in early wounds. In Attempt 2 only 4 wounds were dealt out, of which 2 were even prevented by Soulsword Colossus, so totally different feel of a game compared to the 10 Wounds that were really dealt out in attempt 1 (and I can't remember how many were prevented in that attempt).

Deckbuilding Wise things were also night and Day. In my second attempt getting Miek's rare very early on got me Soulsword Colossus' rare the next turn, as Miek's rare got Wallcrawled. This did take a boosted turn with Super-Skurlls extra draw for next hand, but after that Deck 1 Snowballed hard and got the available copies of Hiroim's 4-costs. Deck 2 was kind of bad in attempt 2, with Corvus Glaive's 2-cost, which stayed recruit only for a long time, without really buying any really good cards. In Attempt 1 Deck 1 was the weaker deck with Mostly Soulsword Colossus and some Hiroim 3-costs, which did work out to get extra bystanders for the first defeated Villain, but as Deck 2 was much stronger with mostly Instinct cards and 4 copies of Corvus Glaive's3-cost the superstar cards of Hiroim's 4-costs went to deck 2 in attempt 1.

Due to the many bystanders from Spider-Infected and some other Villains I ended up with 29 bystanders rescued in attempt 1 (and I believe also 19 KO'ed bystanders from Corvus 3-cost). In attempt 1 Corvus' Uncomoon didn't show up though. In attempt 2 he did, but only 3 Bystanders got KO'ed that game. Hiroim's Transformed 5-cost  was the only really strong card I was able to get in Attempt 1, where it ended up ramping up to 11 attack for one copy. In attempt 2 the rares of Miek and Soulsword Colossus were the heavy hitters, but Hiroim's 5-cost was no joke either eventually even becoming 7 or 8 attack in the final turns.

Both attempts struggled very hard with KOing starers. Even with the KO options from Soulsword Colossus' 3-cost and Hiroim's 6-cost added to Miek's 4-cost, the decks struggled to get rid of Starters and even later in the game there were the dreaded turns with only starters and sometimes even wounds (which is not as bad if you want to Heal anyway). In attempt 1 I got a lot of Wounds too, even with some prevented, but they also did not help the decks get really strong. I noticed again during these games that without KOing a lot of Starters, 2 or 3 attack cards don't really cut it. Or at least,  you don't get reliable and consistent strong hands. That's whjy I was very happy with Hiroim'sd Transformed cards, as they were the welcome exception in my games.

I really enjoy building strong decks, and usually KOing starters is an essential and rewarding as well as fun part of that. WIth most available options just providing 2-3 attack per card, neither of my decks felt really strong, especially in Attempt 1 where I got 0 rares.Maybe I still make the rookie mistake of not buying enough Officers if the HQ lacks recruit Heroes, but my experience has already shifted from focusing too much on recruit and too little on attack (which leads to CIty Management problems) to trying to at least get 1 deck some early attack cards to prevent escapes as much as possible, usually both decks get some early attack cards.

Preventing Wounds from  the Scheme Twists was easier than I'd imagined, as defeating Tim Boo Ba wasn't that hard, but if you managed that early on you'd get a pass on those Wounds for the rest of the game. That the Master Strike could also give Wounds was a rather nasty thing to juggle besides that though. At least the Villains were pretty much Wound-free, excepting Ghost Deadpool. Not a very fun setup for me, as it to me was still a serious challenge, but I really like to be able to KO more. Preventing all escapes proved too hard for me in both games, but I also blame the lack of KO options for the resulting weaker and slower decks.

Kang was pretty beefy, but we got beefy too! Cassie was probably our favorite Hero that we brought along, as she had nice interactions with all of the Henchmen we had to fight. The other heroes were no slouch either, as I leaned into Scott's Heist abilities to get some big attacks going on. Bruce Banner didn't show up in either game, which was disappointing. I guess he still has issues getting the Big Guy to show up =P

Our first game had a terrible starting HQ with two rare and an uncommon (6 cost) as our only 3 slots. Even with the slow start we only had one escape. 
Werewolf by night did fine game one but we found the chip/punch deck just couldn’t produce enough green to hit triggers. 
Game 2 Jentorra came in to fix the lack of green and though her triggers didn’t hit often she allowed Bruce to hit his triggers consistently. Heist also hit well most turns giving a few strong turns.
Interesting game, though I didn't do very well. Blood frenzy took too long to build up in both games and I kept being in awkward positions where I couldn't hit Kang.

Very interesting set up! One players hand really never took off (27 points) but the other hand had some amazing combos occur (74 points!). It took awhile to get Kang though (32 turns! Ugh). Dr strange and Bruce banner were helpful picks. Multi color combo craziness along with black panther and ant man.
Blood Frenzy for the win!

Brent subbing in for Tuba: "That was a blast. Keeping track of the Multiverse, the haunting villains, the expanding/shrinking city, etc. made for a lot of hard strategic decisions. Kang is always a fun challenge, imo."

We always enjoy this scheme - though trying to have no escapes makes it a lot harder depending on the card draw at the start. We added in Ant Army - which we like the Heist ability - even though it can mean for a few extra wounds and we did have to heal once or twice, losing turns. First game we had a lot of scheme twists come out super early - which to start with we liked (expanding the HQ and the City) but then they kept coming and started to shrink it too fast, I think we had 9 out of the 11 Scheme twists in the first half of the villain deck. We didn't see a single ultimate card in the first game which was disappointing or a couple of other cards we were hoping for. We ended up being able to attack the final tactic & final blow Kang with only 1 city/HQ space left. It is always a fun game when it is that close! Our second game we had 4 abomination villains (too expensive to attack) come out first so had an escape really early. The game went downhill from there, but we were able to pull it back a bit but still lost to scheme twists at turn 36 with 3 escapes. We were also happy to have sidekick students only - something we have never tried with before - once we got used to it - it wasn't too bad.
Didnt buy a single Else card, but it worked our since so many villains escaped. Ants/Cons in one hand, multicolored magic in the other!
The required heroes made this an awkward fit. Never felt like we could get ahead

We were not able to win this time but were pretty close. In both games we couldn't get enough attack to hit Kang several turns because of Conqueror, and had to make do with hitting the city or the Multiverse. Bruce Banner pulled his weight in both games, but neither Ant-Man nor Jentorra did enough to pull us through. Elsa Bloodstone was dead weight most of the time, particularly after the city was destroyed, but at least we got to play with new cards and keywords, and the scheme is pretty fun overall.

Finished it by shattering korvac with siryn. Think I got quite lucky on my first attempt, getting groot's uncommon early let me recruit quite a lot of good cards, which helped mitigate the fact that I wasn't getting much ko and a lot of wounds.

Enjoyed the shatter aspect.  Definitely focused completely on Korvac which probably hurt my VP total.  Want to definitely try this again with different heroes.  Also, building up shards with Gamora was a lot of fun and lead to some big hits.

Who is Exodus? The minigame of Korvac Revealed provides a nice twist on standard target the Mastermind play, and it pushed us towards heroes with bankable attack in the form of shards. Maybe this was the trap strategy, and we totally fell for it. But in both of our attempts we had 2 escapes thanks to some aggressive Mr. Sinister clones and we felt pretty lackluster when counting our VP. Hopefully 17 turns is on the faster end this week and can carry our weak VP total.

Yeah, there was no way I was gonna beat Exodus bit hitting him 5 times. I won by fighting the scheme. Got luck with a big hand at the end of the game when it was in Transformed mode. That time I couldn't even hit Exodus once, though I hit him 2 or 3 times in my first try before losing to the scheme.

We started each round with 4 wounds within the first three turns. The most important cards for our plan to defeat the scheme with shards were almost completely in the bottom half of the hero deck. 
This setup looks nice when playing casually, but in competitive it was somewhat exhausting.

Its always sad when both wins come from the Scheme's Special rule not from defeating the MM. Either way a W is a W. I may regret this pick since my other game had 1 less escape and about twice the PPT but I thought it would be foolish to give up 10 turns for that.

column2: Mental Organism Destined to Own a Kaleidoscope
column36:
First game I wanted Nightcrawler to teleport to trigger super powers but it just developed very slowly, and switching to Groot helped manage the recruit side of MODOK much better so I'm going to submit the second game. 

Player one went with a variety of cost deck, focusing on Iron Fist, Groot, and No-Name. Player two went with a spider-friends deck, featuring Scarlet Spider and Spider-Woman.
Player one's deck thinned quickly with no-name and early cytoplasm spikes. One turn I had six different cost cards and Iron Fist just went off. So that was a winning combination.
Player two's deck didn't thin quick enough to prove super effective, but managed the city well enough. I was hoping Spider-Woman would help to set up feasts and it worked alright but didn't get consistent attack or recruit to do severe damage to MODOK or the deck. 

Rating this a 4 because this second game went so smoothly it was a bit boring, overall. But the combinations were fun to find, like Scarlet Spider's Rare "Sting of the Spider" and Spider-Woman's "Radioactive Spider" which forces you to put a card on top of your deck, which would just come back to my hand.

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: My plan to balance recruit and attack worked out well! I always had a use for extra stars - either clearing shards or defeating MODOK’s transformed side.

I really liked this game! The feasts came out great almost every time, had pretty good decks pretty quickly. My strategy was to build recruit-heavy decks so I can buy all the shards from the MM, recruit a lot of heroes and bribe the villains with recruit points. It came out pretty awesome, in the second game I was able to hit the MM 4 times in one turn, that was easily the highlight of both games. Scarlet Spider's 6-costs and Iron Fist's 3-costs were the MVPs for me.

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: I agonized over what heroes to pick for this, and there were a lot to choose from. Sometimes, though, it ends up all being moot. She-Hulk and Grey Hulk were fine choices (though Joe barely showed), but Iron Fist and Brood Queen did all the work. Left hand was just a beast, recruiting both “Living Weapon” and “World-Spanning Hunger”; combined with all the Feast, it didn’t take long after that to bring M.O.D.O.K. down. If it wasn’t for that one pesky Jigsaw that got away…
It was an interesting game but using iron fist and a versatile hero  against modok made the whole game too easy.

First game showered me in shards to the point I was having to spend them to avoid the loss condition. Recruit was an issue because of all the Feast and KO. Tried to address that in the second game, but at least 30% of the hero cards were all Brood Queen, no shard play lead to a slower win. 

Would have liked to try Noghtcrawler/Wolverine(XF) or Gladiator Hulk/Angel for a change of pace.
N/A
Once we got the strategy of focusing on recruit power and using the shards for attack, the game was more comfortable.

Had some really bad luck with the cytoplasm spikes in my second game. 6/10 KOd recruit heroes. By turn 25 or so I reliably had over 10 attack in each hand, but MODOK was stuck on the side where you can only fight with recruit. I wasn't drawing Master Strikes but I did keep drawing Scheme Twists, so MODOK would end up with 15 or 16 attack that I could only get with recruit. I bought the shards off of him, but then I couldn't use them to fight him. Gr. I'm really curious what other heroes other players picked. I tried two totally different strategies in each game, and neither of them really paid off (the bad draws didn't help either). What eventually worked for me was to have lots of recruit in my hand and then tons of shards in the supply. There's got to be a better combo.
(Also, Unite the Shards is one of my favorite schemes. So that brought the experience up to a 3/5)
That was a challenge! Two twists in a row near the end of the game is just brutal.

column2: The Life Aquatic with Heinz Zemo
column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: Very fun setup, had two small hands between all the extra chances to hit henchmen and steve rogers' uncommon

After a miserable first game with twists fateful resurrection filling up the escape pile without me ever getting either of my hands a decent engine, I was pretty sure I was going to be reporting a rare loss.  I'm not a fast player, but I can usually get one of my hands a decent enough engine to win again non-epic masterminds.  My second game was like a song.  I had my right hand down to 3 agents from my opening hand with some wise KOing off of Cape-killers reentering the city on fateful resurrection.  I had the 7 Winter Soldier, the 7 Wiccan and all the rest were Captain America cards for awesome drawing and man out time-ing.  Once the 6-cost cap started rescuing bystanders, Zemo fell pretty quickly.  My left hand got one Zemo kill with the 8-cost Thanos too.  Fun times being able to recruit and actually use the big cost cards.
Defeating villains was difficult, but if we knew what the top card of the villain deck was, we tried to take out as many villains as we could. Dang Fateful Resurrection! Fun times!

It was a good game 1. Game 2 was rough and it really showed me how this scheme is nasty. Certain turns attack was meaningless. Thankfully the Black Swan rare showed up early and was able to manipulate the top of the deck enough to go for the win.

This was rough, but exhilarating.  The first game I got absolutely wrecked by the Scheme-- at the end of turn 14, I had zero cards in both victory piles and I played most of the game one escape away from losing, but I managed to pull it off (didn't help that I accidentally had one too many Twists in the deck...).  Second game went better, but basically along the same lines...and I lost out on some points and gave away an extra escape just because I managed to pull all four Shapeshifted Copycats.  Ah well.

Interesting setup! Was unique how the heroes selected, Mastermind, and Scheme synergized with each other, particularly with Bystanders and KO-ing them. Was unique to include Thanos who benefits from KO-ed Bystanders when other elements of the setup help KO them. I picked Captain America (Falcon) and Hercules because they both helped get Bystanders (which would be essential for Savior and Baron Zemo), and they were both Avengers which gave additional benefits to Baron Zemo Mastermind Tactics. The decks didn't feel the most satisfyingly synergistic, but they functioned well enough to get enough Bystanders for both decks to maintain Savior and avoid wounds from Baron Zemo's Master Strikes. This Scheme was a reminder of how BRUTAL and demoralizing Fateful Resurrection can be. It's devastating to have a full city and be unable to clear it because a ST or MS is on the top of the villain deck with no means of clearing it. The only reprieve was luckily timed Master Strikes (while having rescued Bystanders) which didn't add a villain to the city, or early Scheme Twists before villains had been defeated. Out of curiosity, I tried a second game with Black Swan instead of Hercules with the hope that she would help manage the top of the Villain Deck and Fateful Resurrection. It ended up not mattering, was clunky, and it helped me realize how important Hercules split card (which lets you get 3 fight for discarding a card) was at managing the city. In game 2, I struggled to reliably generate enough fight to manage the city. Game 2 also got wrecked by "rescuing" 2 Shapeshifter Copycat bystanders which pushed villains out of the city. Not my favorite game, but I appreciate the creativity of the setup and how different elements interacted with bystanders.

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: Ha kept filling up towards the end of the game due to the fateful resurrection keyword,I’ll try it again with different heroes

I was surprised to find Thanos was actually very strong to use in this game.  With lots of bystanders it was ideal for him to KO a few now and then!
The Captain and the Devil worked really well with the other Captain America cards.  The standard Captain America "get X for each Hero Class you have" pairs perfectly with Spectrum.
My previous game was with Mastermind - Poison Thanos, and was really hard, so it was a welcome change to have Baron Heinrich Zemo who is a total pinyata :)

Those two matches may have been the worst matches I have ever played. I couldn't get my decks going because of wounds, I didn't have a great HQ, and I couldn't defeat villains because of fateful resurrection. I'm curious if anyone will win this match because it was absolutely brutal for me.

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: Really simple game but the hero setup lead to a lot of really big turns. Storm's Spinning Cyclone was a game changer and all my heroes dished out a ton of damage and recruit - Thor's Odinson and Cyclops's Determination for Recruit, Hulk's common and Groot for attack. Overall the game went really smoothly
The core heroes have a simplified fun factor to them.  It was fun getting back to basics.  The Black Widow engine certainly over-powers any scheme that ups the bystander count.

Our first attempt featured a lot of KO. With the Rogue Uncommon copying either the Black Widow Uncommon or the Thor Rare, I had one hand that dominated while the Hulk hand didn't get much traction. My second game was more even, but Black Widow didn't show up in the HQ until the last few turns and there was much less KOing and so it went slower.

Thought that Women of Vormir (Gamora and Widow) will make this trivial due to bystander and shard synergies. They did help, still both games went long. Second had a nice widow deck in the end. 8 cards and 20 bystanders. Unfortunately one guy got away. (I blame it on the master strike canceling 2-3 consequitve turns in early mid game) Both games had similar score and turn number in the end. First was wound heavy with bit more constant damage. Second got built up in the end with the red widow /gamora and the green Hulk/Groot hand with some of Thor. Codename: T(h)reevengers.  (Keeping an eye out for focusing one hand on hydra villains was important to increase VP. )
my first game was very bad. I made hero changes and it got much better.

First time playing against Supreme Intelligence and Kree Starforce. Haven’t used core heroes for some time so glad to use them again. Had Hawkeye and Black Widow rares which helped in the last few rounds to clear villains and Mastermind.

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: Shards are always fun.  Set up Hulk smash for player 1 and shard accumulation (primarily Gamora) for player 2.
Thanks for running this
Glad I got a second play in. More turns but no escapes.Wolverine and Hulk didn't mesh as well as I hoped.
Black Widow and Storm were born for this scheme and were a lot of fun to use. It was nice to play a setup with mostly core heroes and felt like a good balance of a luck/skill scheme.

I really liked this setup. Spent a lot of time analyzing my hero choices before going with Darkhawk and Domino for a hyperspeed strategy. Not my favorite style of play, but I think it worked out ok this time. I dedicated one hand to those 2 plus quicksilver and the other side stuck with Nightcrawler and Korg. Got pretty lucky in the HQ for game 1 and ended up with Darkhawk's rare and Domino's uncommon by turn 5. I was on easy street the rest of the way with just about every other Player 1 turn making a big number in attack and/or recruit via hyperspeed and with so much Bribe to fight it didn't matter which.
Game 2 was a lot less favorable. The Master Strikes were especially painful in that they replaced several solid hands with a bunch of starter garbage.

Very quick games. I played both in just under an hour lol I hoped my second game would go better because I barely saw any Colossus or Angel the first game, but when I reached turn 28 and got my first escape, I knew I was submitting my first attempt and just stopped right there lol

In game one I focused on Darkhawk and Domino's tech cards supporting each other, and game two I focused on hyperspeed with instinct in one deck and strength in another. Funny enough, both games had the same exact victory point total and turn count, but there was an escape in game two from getting a couple of Streets of NY guys out in a row at the start. I did get to have Quicksilver's rare go off and go through my whole deck though, and that's always very satisfying.

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: This is the 3rd time I've played the Subjugate with Obedience Disks scheme but it's my first victory, so that was good.  We had 5 scheme twists come out fairly early, and by the end we had 8 of them out with twice getting back-to-back twists.  I think I did a pretty good shuffling, so I think it was just a quirk of the deck.  Used Agent Coulson because he's a fave of mine, and Punisher helped clear out some of starters which allowed the Bribe to be used pretty effectively against Kingpin.  All-in-all, a fun game.
Ahhh man I had a fun run the 2nd time, but I had 4 escapes.  Not sure if I should have submitted the second one anyways as that one was won in 22 turns, but either way super fun!

column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: The decision to skip MK heroes really hit the master strikes hard. Had some annoying dead turns. 28 turns was pretty high. The last game really brought my score for this tournament down. I should have replayed last weeks game because of it…

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36:
It may have just been bad luck on my part, but this scheme was quite hard to deal with. I unfortunately managed to get hit with several scheme twists early in the game which made buying cards quite difficult. It was compounded by two hero rares that clogged up the HQ for the entire game. Korg ended up being useless, I never found a good synergy with the other heroes and he never transformed his cards.
This game was familiar since I played against Kingpin using the Mandarin's Rings last tournament as well. I used Punisher then too to good effect. I only had one Master Stike hit without a Marvel Knight to deflect it, but when it did hit the turn was useless. Overall not bad, but the inability to recruit didn't feel good. The only saving grace was the bribe mechanic so my recruit points could be used for something at least.
Heroes did not jive. Spent more time shuffling and not recruiting than anything else.

The first game went really bad with the villain deck getting front loaded with scheme twists. One players' deck took longer to come together. Well, we thought the second game could not be worse. We play the second one, and as fate would have it, BOOM! Literally the TOP THREE cards are all scheme twists. I thought I was dreamin'. Somehow though we pulled off a better game in the second, however.

I took BTs advice and choose two heroes that I thought would work and then assessed their cohesiveness by separating the hero deck into sides to check synergy.... bit laborious but boy did it work. Recruited some officers early to offset the "discs" and with bribe KP was a sitting duck.
Probably the most palatable version of Superhuman Baseball possible. Also, I think this setup made me realize that I have been underrating Wolfsbane. She kicked butt in this game.
I had a lot of fun playing this setup. Thank you!

column36: Had some great luck with my scheme twists. 3 of them flipped locations and one flipped a master strike with no one on 3rd. All the hero KO options really helped with the hyperspeed build.

This was a fun one. I think the location villains helped to balance out not having two locations in the city for villains to move to. The rival overlords were a cool villain group. I like that they got weaker with every other rival overlord on the board. I got smoked in the first game because of the luck of the draw in the villain deck. But the second game was way more balanced. Captain Carter hits hard!
Interesting scheme
A win is a win.

Darkhawk is my favorite character of all time. I recruited his rare early and was able to win woth Hyperspeed. I didn't have time for a second match , but I would have substituted Mr. FANTASTIC for War Machine. However... Mr Fantastic did finish off Hood with Ultimate Nullifier. Im just mad I left points on the table as the Hoods Warehouse Tactic remained a location.

column36: I've always hated this scheme.  My son decided to join me and had never experienced superhuman baseball.  He had the instinct deck as player 1 and really helped control the city / field.  I had the strength / tech deck and got in more big turns.  I'll be happy to move this scheme to my completed schemes pile and never look at it again!  Thanks for making this setup play-able!  I'm amazed you pulled that off.

column2: I Prey the Stones My Soul to Keep
column36: I really enjoyed the decks that I ended up with. I had a wound based Bruce/Torch deck that did solid and a odd-cost M/Wanda & Vision deck that popped off
Game 1 I had no rares show up in the HQ.

Not a lot of VP but 21 turns is hella fast. Got lucky with the tactic order and got some decent Human Torch and Mr. Fantastic going early, with few twists ocurring. That one escape will hurt though I'm sure, but my second attempt was far worse lol.

What a set up! The Stones kept me on my toes the whole time with the combo of Stonekeeper and Soul Stone going off first game while the Sentinels kept it powered up. First time with Prey and it was a lot more fun than I thought it could be with all of the strategy in picking who gets what on ties.

Excellent set up, best Pod I've had all tourney

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: Some really clever interactions in this setup! The better hand focused on Wanda's interactions with odd-cost cards. The other hand focused on getting cards with higher attack and recruit values to bump up Black Cat's Pickpocket, although that ended up working better for the Wanda deck with Vision's phasing abilities, especially having their rare card. She pickpocketed 12 attack one time that way! Human Torch ruined things a little by filling the other deck with wounds, on top of the wounds coming from Lady Deathstrike every time she Preyed. I counted 7 or more wounds in that deck and once healed to KO 4 of them! I must have hit her Cybernetic Healing Factor tactic 5 or 6 times too. Overall, it was a great game for me.

Man, we couldn't line up the KO's with Invisible woman which was sad. Both attempts we picked up Flame On and that helped city management. Both games had 1 turn that got out of hand and push 1 card out... very frustrating...Hopefully we made up for it in turns, but I doubt it...
Wandavision stole the show this game, Invisible Woman got to many wounds and couldnt focus them off fast enough

the heroes made it dificult,
didnt like how game one went so swapped siryn for invisible woman
but smh did worse the 2nd try
i feel cursed in this tournament at this point...
